# Generated PMX fixtures

`minimal-loader-smoke.pmx` is a deterministic, redistribution-safe PMX generated by `scripts/fixtures/generate-minimal-pmx.mjs`; it represents a tiny texture-free tetrahedron-like skinned mesh with one material, three bones, and one vertex morph.

Generated PMX files include MMD-compatible special display frames (`Root` and `表情`) so they can be opened by MikuMikuDance for dumper oracle generation.

`rest-pose/*.pmx` fixtures are generated by the same script with `--all-rest-pose`; they cover append rotate, append local, IK chain, fixed/local axis, and transform-after-physics bone metadata for rest-pose identity regression tests.

`visual/*.pmx` fixtures are generated by the same script with `--all-visual`; they cover small redistribution-safe PMX scenes for visual regression paths. `mmd-material-order-body-outline-interleave.pmx` contains two overlapping angled translucent edged thin boxes split across two PMX materials; `mmd-outline-normal-silhouette.pmx` contains one rotated translucent edged box for outline normal/depth visibility checks; `mmd-texture-alpha-used-uv-cutout.pmx` contains one textured opaque PMX material whose used UVs sample alpha cutout pixels; `mmd-texture-alpha-atlas-padding-ignored.pmx` contains one textured opaque PMX material whose used UVs stay inside an opaque atlas region while unused padding is transparent.

`visual/mmd-viewer-background-room.pmx` is the main-viewer background fixture. Its textured floor and wall use a saturated generated checker texture, and its small black prop is intentionally black. It is generated by `--visual-case mmd-viewer-background-room` for the independent native-WebGPU background gate.

`visual/mmd-viewer-self-shadow-receiver.pmx` is the main-viewer native-WebGPU self-shadow fixture. It contains a wide, PMX self-shadow-enabled receiver and a separate floating caster. The gate captures SelfShadow OFF/ON from two fixed camera views, asserts receiver-plane luminance darkening, and compares the ray-projected world-space darkening centroid across the camera-only move.
